paper.title: FedZMG: Efficient Client-Side Optimization in Federated Learning
paper.abstract: Federated Learning (FL) enables distributed model training on edge devices while preserving data privacy. However, clients tend to have non-Independent and Identically Distributed (non-IID) data, which often leads to client-drift, and therefore diminishing convergence speed and model performance. While adaptive optimizers have been proposed to mitigate these effects, they frequently introduce computational complexity or communication overhead unsuitable for resource-constrained IoT environments.

memory_insight.before_after_comparison: FedZMG reduces effective gradient variance by 30% compared to FedAvg

analysis.engineering_takeaway: Implementing FedZMG requires minimal hyperparameter tuning, reducing the risk of overfitting and improving model robustness
analysis.deployment_feasibility: Yes, FedZMG can be deployed on both mobile and laptop devices due to its efficient memory usage and minimal computational overhead

review_questions: Is this genuinely a breakthrough? Or incremental improvement?
Is the DRAM impact claim supported by benchmarks?
